title: Second-Generation PDAs: It's High Time for Wireless
author: Isaac Ro
source_file: 2002 Second-Generation PDAs_ It_s High Time for Wireless.pdf
abstract: Aberdeen Group assessed the PDA market in early 2002 and argued that first-generation devices (focused on personal information management) were insufficient amid the IT spending downturn. The study predicts that 2002 will be the year vendors deliver integrated wireless PIM and e-mail functionality in a secure enterprise manner. Aberdeen reviews wireless PDA devices from Palm, Handspring, and Microsoft and highlights the impending arrival of packaged enterprise mobility solutions.
importance_rationale: Early and accurate characterization of the enterprise mobility inflection point: the transition from standalone PDAs to wirelessly-connected enterprise devices that would eventually lead to modern smartphones.
relevance_rationale: The enterprise mobile device adoption patterns, ROI framing, and integration challenges analyzed in 2002 directly parallel smartphone enterprise deployment dynamics still relevant today.
prescience_rationale: The prediction that 2002 would mark the transition to integrated wireless enterprise PDAs proved correct: the Handspring Treo and BlackBerry accelerated this shift. Palm was acquired by HP in 2010; the smartphone paradigm Aberdeen anticipated became universal by 2007-2010.
